steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which statement the author would most likely disagree with. Find the author’s core claim about vocal manipulation in hyperpop, then look for the choice that contradicts it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It is a symbol of the influence that hyperpop exerts on listeners’ sense of self.’: The passage links vocal manipulation to identity and how technology mediates human experience — consistent with the author’s view. The author would not disagree. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It represents changes to human experience brought about by technology.’: The author explicitly frames vocal manipulation as commentary on how digital technology mediates human experience — this aligns with the passage. The author would not disagree. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It is an aesthetic feature that has little or no social import.’: The author states vocal manipulation is ‘not an arbitrary stylistic choice’ and functions as meaningful cultural commentary — directly contradicting the claim that it has little or no social import. The author would disagree. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It confirms hyperpop’s ability to offset certain negative effects of digital technology.’: The passage does not claim vocal manipulation offsets negative effects of technology — this falls outside the author’s argument, but the author never contradicts it either. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
